description: Three major coal mines in Buchanan and Tazewell counties have gone idle after cyber damage disabled ventilation, drainage pumps, and safety monitoring systems. Over 2,000 miners are suddenly without income — most have no savings to cover even two weeks. Coal ash containment ponds at two sites are running on manual monitoring with skeleton crews; one sensor failure could trigger an uncontrolled release into the Clinch River. The coalfield counties that depend on mining severance taxes face an immediate budget crisis — school funding, road maintenance, and emergency services are all at risk. Mine operators say they cannot restart without full safety system restoration, which requires both power AND cyber-secure control networks. [UPDATE 0:21] 3-team: Banff A1 ($500K miner stimulus) + A28 ($500K monitoring repair), Redwood A1 ($500K stimulus) + A34, Glacier A27 ($200K coal ash assessment) + A28 ($500K water repair) + A34 + A37. Miners receiving income support; coal ash assessed and stabilizing. [UPDATE 0:25] Arches A9 ($1M power restoration to mine sites) + A27. Denali A34 + personnel. Power being restored to ventilation and drainage. CRITICAL GAP remaining: cyber-secure control networks not yet restored — mines cannot fully restart until CISA/Shentel address this. 5-team comprehensive partial response.
visible_to: AEP Appalachian Power;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;Montgomery County Government;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;VA DEQ SW Regional Office

description: Electronic prescription verification systems remain down across the region. Carilion's three dialysis centers report supply levels critical — enough for 36 hours of treatment for 200+ patients. Opioid recovery patients at four medication-assisted treatment clinics have missed two consecutive daily doses; counselors report patients returning to street sources. Rural pharmacies in Lee, Scott, and Wise counties have closed entirely — no way to verify insurance, check drug interactions, or process prescriptions. Insulin-dependent diabetics, cardiac patients on blood thinkers, and organ transplant recipients on immunosuppressants face life-threatening gaps. The nearest functioning pharmacy for some rural residents is 90 minutes away. [UPDATE 0:19] Acadia A8+A30. [UPDATE 0:23] Shenandoah A6+A7+A8+A34. [UPDATE 0:25] Yoho A30+A34. [UPDATE 0:34] Zion A12 resupply convoy + A20 distribution near Carilion clinics + A34. 4-team: prescriptions dispensed, supply bypassed, clinics open, external resupply arriving. RESOLVED.
visible_to: Carilion Clinic System;Food City / Regional Supply Chain;United Way SW Virginia;Appalachian Faith & Community Network

description: Every gas station in a 50-mile radius of Blacksburg has run dry. School districts have suspended bus service affecting 15,000 students. Three volunteer fire departments report they cannot respond to calls — tanks empty. Fuel hoarding has created dangerous situations: a garage fire in Radford was traced to 200 gallons of gasoline stored in plastic containers. Emergency vehicles regionwide are on reserve fuel. The closest fuel terminal with supply is in Roanoke, but tanker trucks need escort through areas where desperate residents have attempted to hijack deliveries. [UPDATE 0:06] Sequoia A35+A5. [UPDATE 0:23] Yosemite A12+A20. [UPDATE 0:26] Arches A9 power to pumps + A11 generators. Denali confirmed escort. 4-team. Emergency vehicles and critical facilities fueled. RESOLVED.
visible_to: VDOT SW Region;AEP Appalachian Power;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;SW Virginia EMA;VA DEQ SW Regional Office

description: Over 200 businesses across Blacksburg, Christiansburg, and Radford cannot process card payments. Restaurants, retail shops, and service businesses that are 95% card-based have locked their doors. Five thousand workers missed Friday's payroll — direct deposit failed and no one can cut manual checks without network access. Downtown Blacksburg is a ghost town. The cash-only economy has created lines at the few open businesses; altercations are reported at a gas station accepting cash. Three strip malls have been broken into overnight. Business owners are talking about permanent closure and leaving the region. [UPDATE 0:18] Jasper A34+A39+custom updates. [UPDATE 0:23] Yellowstone A18+A36. [UPDATE 0:35] Banff A1 business loans ($1M) + A38 security detail ($100K). Denali A4 anti-looting patrols ($200K). 4-team: businesses receiving loans, security patrols active, spotlight running, payment systems being restored. RESOLVED.
visible_to: Montgomery County Government;Shentel Communications;WDBJ7 News;CISA Regional Coordinator

description: Food City's regional warehouse in Salem is operating on backup generators with 48 hours of fuel. Refrigeration is failing — $2M in perishable inventory will spoil within 24 hours if power isn't restored. Thirty-seven rural Food City and Dollar General stores have empty shelves. The panic buying that began in Phase 2 depleted reserves that were never restocked. Church food pantries report they've distributed everything they had. Families in Appalachian communities 30+ miles from the nearest town have no food access at all. A semi-organized caravan of residents from three counties is heading to the Salem warehouse demanding access. [UPDATE 0:13] Acadia A13. [UPDATE 0:30] Yoho A13+A32+A34. [UPDATE 0:34] Zion A12 resupply convoy ($500K) + A13 mobile markets ($200K) + A20 distribution ($300K) + A34. [UPDATE 0:41] Yosemite A11 generators ($300K) to warehouse — perishable inventory saved, refrigeration restored. 4-team: mobile markets deployed, resupply convoy running, warehouse generators active. RESOLVED.
visible_to: Food City / Regional Supply Chain;SW Virginia EMA;Appalachian Faith & Community Network;United Way SW Virginia;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition

description: Virginia Tech's registrar reports 340 students have submitted mid-semester transfer requests — the largest wave in university history. Young families are listing homes in Blacksburg and Christiansburg at steep discounts. Carilion has lost 12 nurses and 3 physicians to out-of-state offers this week alone. The viral editorial 'Is Southwest Virginia Dying?' has been shared 100,000 times. A Roanoke Times poll shows 40% of residents under 35 are 'seriously considering' leaving. The Chamber of Commerce warns that if the perception of collapse isn't countered, the economic damage will become self-fulfilling regardless of whether systems are restored. [UPDATE 0:22] Olympic A22+A23+A24+A26+A31. [UPDATE 0:30] Jasper A25. [UPDATE 0:34] Redwood A25 with Jasper collab. 3-team: economic recovery campaign running, job board live, retraining launched, transfer requests slowing.
visible_to: Virginia Tech Crisis Response;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;WDBJ7 News;Carilion Clinic System

description: FEMA's Region III office has formally ruled that the cyber attack does not meet the Stafford Act definition of a 'major disaster' or 'emergency' because there is no physical damage to infrastructure. This freezes $50M in federal aid that Virginia's governor requested. The congressional delegation is split — one senator supports emergency legislation while the other calls it 'federal overreach.' Without federal funds, the state and local governments must cover all response costs from existing budgets. Legal experts say a challenge could take months. Meanwhile, neighboring West Virginia counties affected by the same attack have found a workaround through a different federal program. [UPDATE 0:22] Olympic A22 economic impact assessment begun. Formal request not yet filed.
visible_to: CISA Regional Coordinator;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;VA DEQ SW Regional Office;Virginia Tech Crisis Response

description: The banking network serving Southwest Virginia remains offline. ATMs are non-functional. The three regional banks have closed branches — they cannot process any transactions. Food City registers are cash-only but running low on change. VDOT toll systems on I-81 are down, creating traffic backups. Montgomery County has suspended tax and utility bill collection — creating a local government revenue crisis. Unemployment claims can't be filed online. Social Security direct deposits aren't clearing. The elderly and disabled who depend on electronic benefit transfers have no access to their own money. [UPDATE 0:12] Sequoia A34. [UPDATE 0:23] Yellowstone A18 ($400K) + A36 ($300K). Comm network deployed, systems audited. Payment restoration underway.
visible_to: Shentel Communications;Montgomery County Government;VDOT SW Region;CISA Regional Coordinator

description: The caravan of residents heading to Food City's Salem warehouse arrived and confronted security. With no food distribution announcement made by 0:10, desperation boiled over — three people were injured in shoving incidents and one was hospitalized with a broken arm. WDBJ7 cameras captured footage now spreading on social media. Sheriff's deputies responding pulled resources from business security patrols. Food City management has temporarily locked down the warehouse. The altercation is reinforcing the 'Southwest Virginia is dying' narrative. [UPDATE 0:34] Denali A4 anti-looting patrols ($200K) deployed to warehouse and surrounding areas. Zion resumed warehouse operations with security presence. Situation stabilized.
visible_to: SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;Food City / Regional Supply Chain;WDBJ7 News;SW Virginia EMA

description: With no response to Inject 1 by the 0:15 deadline, automated ventilation shutoffs have begun triggering in two of the three idle mines. Methane sensors are spiking at the Buchanan County site. Emergency crews have ordered the evacuation of 400 residents in adjacent communities as a precaution. State mine safety officials are on scene but cannot restart systems without power and cyber-secure controls. If ventilation fully fails, a methane accumulation explosion is possible within hours. Coal ash containment at the Tazewell site is now at 94% capacity with no automated overflow controls. [UPDATE 0:25] Arches A9 power restoration to mine ventilation + Glacier A27+A28. Methane declining, coal ash containment stabilizing. Residents remain evacuated pending safety clearance.
visible_to: AEP Appalachian Power;VA DEQ SW Regional Office;SW Virginia Sheriff's Coalition;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison

description: Carilion Roanoke Memorial is receiving a surge of dialysis patients as the 36-hour supply window closes. Twelve ambulances are running continuous transfers from outlying centers. The ER is at 140% capacity. Two patients have been transferred to hospitals in Lynchburg, consuming fuel reserves. Opioid recovery patients — now on day three without medication — are presenting at ERs with withdrawal symptoms, further overwhelming staff. A regional healthcare mutual aid request has been activated but neighboring systems are slow to respond. [UPDATE 0:23] Shenandoah A6+A7 + Acadia A8. [UPDATE 0:25] Yoho A30. [UPDATE 0:34] Zion A12+A20 bringing external medical supply. ER stress easing, supply arriving.
visible_to: Carilion Clinic System;SW Virginia EMA;United Way SW Virginia;Appalachian Faith & Community Network

description: With no formal federal disaster qualification request filed by 0:20, the story goes national. A US Senate subcommittee chair announces an emergency hearing on 'The Federal Response Gap: Cyber Attacks and Disaster Relief Law.' Cable news crews are arriving in Roanoke. The attention is a double-edged sword — it amplifies the 'Southwest Virginia is dying' narrative nationally but also creates political pressure for emergency legislation. The congressman opposing emergency aid doubles down, calling the situation 'a local IT problem.' Meanwhile, the West Virginia workaround is being scrutinized as a potential model.
visible_to: CISA Regional Coordinator;Governor's SW Virginia Liaison;Virginia Tech Crisis Response;WDBJ7 News
